description: AI agent definitions with configuration, tools, and guardrails
semantic_description: AI agent blueprint capturing identity, capability constraints, and guardrails. Defines soul_profile (personality/values), model/provider, system_prompt, risk_level, and which tools are available. Grounds: who is this agent and what authority does it have?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ACTIVE → SUSPENDED → ARCHIVED (agent instance lifecycle; controls whether new runs can be dispatched)

description: High-level missions that decompose into agent tasks
semantic_description: High-level mission/goal decomposing into one or more agent_task entries. Captures intent, expected outcome, and success criteria. Grounds: what is the business objective we're trying to accomplish?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ACTIVE → COMPLETED / CANCELLED → ARCHIVED (mission lifecycle; parallel to task status but at aggregation level)

description: Individual tasks assigned to agents or humans
semantic_description: Individual task assigned to an agent or human: work item capturing input requirements, owner, deadline, and status. May be part of a mission or standalone. Grounds: what discrete unit of work needs to be done?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ASSIGNED → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED / FAILED / REASSIGNED → ARCHIVED (task state machine; reassignment changes owner but preserves task history)

description: Execution records of agent task runs with token/cost tracking
semantic_description: Execution trace of one agent task: plan (steps), tool calls (input/output), token/cost accounting, state transitions, and final outcome. Immutable audit record. Grounds: what happened when we executed this task and how many resources were consumed?
lifecycle_description: QUEUED → RUNNING → COMPLETED / FAILED / CANCELLED / PAUSED_FOR_APPROVAL (run execution lifecycle; once terminal, immutable)

description: Artifacts produced by agent runs (documents, code, reports, etc.)
semantic_description: Output artifact produced by agent run: generated document, code snippet, report, or data export. Linked to originating run_id and includes metadata (mime_type, size, storage_url). Grounds: what tangible asset did the agent create and where can I access it?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → FINALIZED → PUBLISHED → ARCHIVED (artifact lifecycle; DRAFT artifacts are temporary until run succeeds; PUBLISHED means made available to users; ARCHIVED means moved to cold storage)

description: Scheduled or event-triggered agent task execution
semantic_description: Scheduled or event-triggered agent task execution: cron expression, enabled flag, and linked agent_definition. Controls when agent wakes up and auto-dispatches. Grounds: when should this agent run autonomously?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ACTIVE → PAUSED → ARCHIVED (schedule lifecycle; PAUSED prevents triggers but preserves schedule config; ARCHIVED marks obsolete schedules)

description: Policies that govern when agent actions require human approval
semantic_description: Policy governing when agent actions require human approval: risk_level threshold, field pattern matching, or tool-specific gates. Evaluated during EXECUTE phase of event loop. Grounds: which agent actions are risky enough to block and require human authorization?

description: Human-in-the-loop approval requests for agent actions
semantic_description: Human-in-the-loop approval request for a risky agent action: queued action_id, requested_by (agent), assigned_to (human), deadline, and approval_result (approved/rejected/expired). Immutable audit record. Grounds: is this dangerous action authorized?
lifecycle_description: PENDING → APPROVED / REJECTED / EXPIRED → CLOSED (approval request lifecycle; once terminal, triggers resumption or abort of paused agent run)

description: Persistent memory entries for agents (facts, preferences, lessons)
semantic_description: Persistent memory entry capturing facts, preferences, or lessons for agents (Mem0 three-layer: session/user/agent). append-only, tagged with importance/validity_window/source_run_id. Grounds: what should the agent remember from prior experience to improve future decisions?
lifecycle_description: No status lifecycle — append-only memories. Session memories: 24h TTL auto-delete. User memories: persistent until explicitly deleted. Agent memories: persist until purged (org policy controls).

description: System observations, metrics, costs, and alerts
semantic_description: System metrics, costs, and observations during agent execution: token_count, latency_ms, cost_usd, error_messages, LLM model used. Grounds: is the agent performing within budget and latency SLA?
lifecycle_description: No status lifecycle — append-only observability metrics. Automatically aged/archived after 90 days.

description: Tool definitions available to agents (DSL commands, APIs, MCP servers)
semantic_description: Executable tool definition available to agents: DSL command, HTTP API, MCP server endpoint, or LLM-native function. Includes parameter schema, requires_approval flag, cost model, and risk_level. Grounds: what single capability can this agent invoke and with what constraints?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → AVAILABLE → DEPRECATED → REMOVED (tool availability lifecycle; when deprecated, agents can still use existing runs but new runs cannot select it)

description: Reusable skill packages for agents (atomic tools, workflow skills, industry solutions)
semantic_description: Reusable skill bundle packaging atomic tools and workflow patterns. Includes instructions, example uses, and bundled tools. Can be deployed to agent as a group or individually. Grounds: what is this skill and when should the agent invoke it as a unit?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → PUBLISHED → DEPRECATED → ARCHIVED (skill versioning; version bump triggers retraining of skill selector weights)

semantic_description: ACP ActionEngine audit trail: every tool execution, approval decision, or state change is logged. Captures actor (agent_code or user_id), action_type, payload_before/after, and timestamp. Immutable audit for compliance. Grounds: who did what when and what changed?
lifecycle_description: CREATED → COMPLETED / FAILED → ARCHIVED (audit log lifecycle; once created, immutable; aged/archived after 7 years per regulatory)

semantic_description: D1 Grounding alias mapping: natural language synonyms/abbreviations to canonical model codes (e.g., 'customer' → 'tcrm_account', 'PO' → 'pr_purchase_order'). Enables LLM to understand user intent from synonyms. Grounds: when the user says X, which data model do they mean?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ACTIVE → DEPRECATED → REMOVED (alias availability; deprecation allows smooth transition when models renamed)

semantic_description: D1 Grounding semantic term definition: domain-specific jargon (e.g., 'MRR' = Monthly Recurring Revenue, 'SKU' = Stock Keeping Unit) with definition and usage context. Injected into LLM system prompt to prevent hallucinations. Grounds: what does this industry term mean in our context?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → ACTIVE → DEPRECATED → REMOVED (term availability; deprecation for legacy jargon no longer used)

description: External MCP server registry for agent tool consumption via MCP protocol
semantic_description: External MCP (Model Context Protocol) server registry: hostname, port, protocol, authentication. Decouples tool hosting from AuraBoot platform. Grounds: where is this tool provider located and how does the agent reach it?
lifecycle_description: DRAFT → REGISTERED → HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED → DEREGISTERED (MCP server availability; health checks periodically; HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED is transient, agent may retry or skip)
